highlights.title: SoftBank Shares Tumble After Report of OpenAI’s IPO Delay
highlights.description: SoftBank Group Corp.’s stock fell the most since August 2024 on concerns that OpenAI may hold off on an initial public offering until next year and delay returns for its Japanese backer.

highlights.title: Apple adds Google Gemini coding assistant in Xcode 26.6 update
highlights.description: Apple has released Xcode 26.6, adding support for a third coding assistant to the company’s integrated development environment software.

highlights.title: AI Cost Reality Check Hits Asia Tech Stocks as Apple Hikes Price
highlights.description: Asian technology stocks slumped after Apple Inc. and Microsoft Corp. raised prices for their products, stoking concern that rising component costs will curb demand for devices and eventually slow the memory chip rally that has powered much of the AI trade.

highlights.title: Anthropic Thinks Its Own Success Is Key to Making AI Safe
highlights.description: Anthropic's critics argue it's rapidly accumulating power. The company says that's what responsible AI development looks like.

highlights.title: Tumbling AI stocks signal another day of turmoil for tech companies
highlights.description: SoftBank Group plunged, leading a broad selloff in Asian technology stocks amid mounting concerns over the rising cost of artificial intelligence infrastructure.

highlights.title: Goldman: Could Make Sense to Diversify Away From Chipmakers
highlights.description: Christian Mueller-Glissmann, managing director for portfolio strategy and asset allocation at Goldman Sachs, discusses the tech selloff and why he thinks it could make sense to diversify away from semiconductor makers and lean more toward hyperscalers. "These are incredibly cyclical companies," Mueller-Glissmann tells Bloomberg Television. "So to some extent, it could make sense to diversify a bit away from them." (Source: Bloomberg)

highlights.title: GCash Owner Said to Seek $1.5 Billion in Record Philippine IPO
highlights.description: The company behind the Philippines’ biggest mobile wallet GCash is preparing what may become the country’s biggest initial public offering ever, seeking to raise as much as 92.3 billion pesos ($1.5 billion).

highlights.title: OHB Shares Dip After German Satellite Firm’s €789 Million Re-IPO
highlights.description: Executives from German satellite firm OHB SE and KKR & Co. rang the opening bell at the Frankfurt Stock Exchange on Friday after they raised €789 million in a share sale to increase investors’ access to the space company.

highlights.title: Apple Shares Fall After Prices Increase for Macs, iPads
highlights.description: Apple Inc. shares fell after it raised prices of all Macs, iPads, home devices and the Vision Pro to offset cost hikes caused by a shortage of memory chips and storage. The price increases are in effect globally and are largely unprecedented, with no equivalent in the company's modern history of sweeping hikes across much of its product line. Bloomberg's Neil Campling reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

highlights.title: Samsung, SK Hynix Reportedly Preparing Huge AI Spending Push
highlights.description: Samsung Electronics Co. and SK Hynix Inc. are preparing to announce hundreds of billions of dollars worth in new investments on Monday, according to South Korean media reports this week.

ai.title: 9to5Mac Daily: June 26, 2026 – Apple announces significant price increases
ai.description:
Listen to a recap of the top stories of the day from 9to5Mac. 9to5Mac Daily is available on iTunes and Apple’s Podcasts app, Stitcher, TuneIn, Google Play, or through our dedicated RSS feed for Overcast and other podcast players.
Sponsored by Roborock: Check out Roborock’s Prime Day Sale featuring up to $1,500 off best-selling robot vacuums and wet dry vacuums, including the Saros 10R, Qrevo Edge 2, Qrevo Curv 2 Flow, and more.

ai.title: European Stocks Fall on Renewed Tech Woes; Zalando Declines
ai.description: European shares fell from record levels amid shifting sentiment toward artificial intelligence and technology stocks.

ai.title: Brutal Week of Swings Leaves Korea’s AI Stock Rally in Turmoil
ai.description: South Korean stocks went from euphoria to anxiety in a gut-wrenching week that exposed just how fragile the AI rally has become.

ai.title: The AI Trade Is No Longer About Owning One Thing: Taking Stock
ai.description: The case for the artificial-intelligence trade remains intact, but the risk of getting it wrong has risen considerably. This week showed just how unforgiving the theme has become.

chips_cloud.title: Goldman: Could Make Sense to Diversify Away From Chipmakers
chips_cloud.description: Christian Mueller-Glissmann, managing director for portfolio strategy and asset allocation at Goldman Sachs, discusses the tech selloff and why he thinks it could make sense to diversify away from semiconductor makers and lean more toward hyperscalers. "These are incredibly cyclical companies," Mueller-Glissmann tells Bloomberg Television. "So to some extent, it could make sense to diversify a bit away from them." (Source: Bloomberg)

chips_cloud.title: Korean Stocks Plunge 9% on Chip Selloff
chips_cloud.description: South Korean stocks were hit by a second trading suspension this week as chipmakers plunged, highlighting the market’s sensitivity to swings in global artificial-intelligence sentiment. The benchmark Kospi extended losses after the Korea Exchange’s 20 minute trading halt, falling as much as 9%. Bloomberg's Anthony Stephens reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

business.title: GCash Owner Said to Seek $1.5 Billion in Record Philippine IPO
business.description: The company behind the Philippines’ biggest mobile wallet GCash is preparing what may become the country’s biggest initial public offering ever, seeking to raise as much as 92.3 billion pesos ($1.5 billion).

business.description: Executives from German satellite firm OHB SE and KKR & Co. rang the opening bell at the Frankfurt Stock Exchange on Friday after they raised €789 million in a share sale to increase investors’ access to the space company.

ev_autonomous.title: More Expensive iPhones on the Horizon?
ev_autonomous.description: Bloomberg Intelligence says Apple has to hike prices for its iPhones after raising prices for its Macs, iPads and home devices. Those hikes went into effect globally on Thursday. Bloomberg Intelligence Senior Technology Analyst Anurag Rana spoke with Bloomberg's Yvonne Man and Avril Hong on "Bloomberg: The China Show." (Source: Bloomberg)

regulation.title: VW Eyes as Many as 100,000 Job Cuts
regulation.description: Volkswagen is looking to cut tens of thousands of additional jobs and may shutter factories in a push by CEO Oliver Blume to make Europe’s biggest automaker more competitive, according to a report in Manager Magazin. Bloomberg's Elisabeth Behrmann has more. (Source: Bloomberg)
